meaning: adj.豐｡譛蛾仆遒咲噪,荳榊女螯ｨ遒咲噪
examples: I hoped hang gliding would deliver the unencumbered essence of flight.
So far, so conventional. At the end of July last year, the Fed had $760 billion of unencumbered Treasuries, or 87% of its assets.
No wonder, then, that those who build with glass look forward to a day when their structures will be unencumbered by metal or other materials.
Mayoral candidates, on the other hand, can run and win as independents, unencumbered by irrelevant issues.
All of these efforts set up PSEG for unencumbered growth when demand picks up.
